_comment: Copy to node.local.json (gitignored) and fill in your own paths. Placeholders {hub_root}/{pid}/{cache_root}/{sid}/{YYYY-MM} resolve at runtime. The watcher (src/audio_inbox_watch.py) reads this file.
_transcribe_cli: optional: path to media_transcribe_cli.py; defaults to the one next to the watcher
_sources_note: discover=project-inboxes scans {hub_root}/_inbox AND {hub_root}/<pid>/_<pid>_inbox/ AND {hub_root}/<pid>/ top-level files; pid = the <pid> folder name. For a single flat inbox instead use: {"root":"{hub_root}/_inbox","route":"mapper","claim":true}.

outputs._voiceprints_note: project_projection = where the per-project speaker registry (index.json + profiles/ name cards WITHOUT vectors + members.json) lives; set to {hub_root}/{pid} to keep it at the project root. local_cache = node-local dir for the embeddings store (voiceprints.json), kept OFF the shared hub. global_registry = SHARED canonical embeddings store on the hub ({hub_root}/_voiceprints/profiles/{voice_hash}.json + registry.json), so an enroll on one node is readable by others; open (not encrypted) and kept out of git via .gitignore. Omit global_registry to keep embeddings node-local only. All active only when voiceprint_mode != off (see below).

_voiceprint_mode_note: off = diarization only (SPEAKER_00/01…); enroll = build the project registry from new voices (first bootstrap pass); match = identify speakers against the existing registry. Requires speaker_mode: diarize + HF token.
_ktalk_note: A Ktalk (Kontur.Talk) download is a media file + a transcript naming the speakers. When one is found next to the media, speakers are taken from it and diarization is skipped (ASR still runs). {stem} = media file name without extension; [] disables the lookup. See docs/speaker-recognition.md.
_cleanup_note: keep the hub tidy. copy_asr_chunk_artifacts:true is the standard mode — it persists per-chunk WAV/JSON so an interrupted long run can resume the finished chunks instead of re-ASR from scratch. It is free on disk because cleanup_intermediates:true prunes those chunks (+ raw/merged dumps + the asr/ scratch dir) after a SUCCESSFUL run; the chunks survive only when a run failed, which is exactly when recovery/resume needs them. Set copy_asr_chunk_artifacts:false only to disable crash-recovery entirely.
_source_disposition_note: OPT-IN inbox cleanup for the SOURCE recordings (transcripts/outputs are never touched). on_asr_done: 'leave' (default) keeps the source + sidecars in place, as today. 'move' relocates the source audio and ALL its sidecars (.state.json, .processed-asr, .claim.json, .asr-log.txt) into a scanner-skipped {source_root}/{processed_dir_name}/ AFTER success — a mirror of _failed/. Nothing is deleted; the session still shows in _sessions-index.md. on_asr_done_after_days is a safety delay: move only once the file is that many days past finished_at (fractional = hours, e.g. 0.25 = 6h; 0 = same sweep).
_scan_knobs: which files the watcher picks up; omit any key to use built-in defaults
_cloud_watchdog: abort a wedged CloudStorage syscall — macOS Google Drive (File Provider) can hang a recursive listdir/stat/open on a dataless subtree indefinitely at 0% CPU, with no Errno of its own. scan_op_timeout_seconds bounds each per-source discovery scan (wedged source is logged + skipped); cloud_op_timeout_seconds bounds the per-file pickup. Make a source available-offline to include one that keeps timing out. No-op on Windows (no SIGALRM).
_scheduling_knobs: CPU-aware deferral + retry policy + optional time window
_multi_node: opt-in cross-machine coordination via per-file claim.json
